deny-k-alias.description: Blocks the popular 'k' alias for kubectl — catches all 'k ...' invocations regardless of subcommand. Install separately from kubectl-* to guard the alias even when kubectl itself is allowed. Requires bash-deny and jq on PATH; no-ops if either is missing.

deny-kubectl-create.description: Blocks new resource creation: kubectl create, apply, replace, run. Requires bash-deny and jq on PATH; no-ops if either is missing.
deny-kubectl-create.shell: CMD=$(printf '%s' "$PI_TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r '.command // empty'); [ -z "$CMD" ] || bash-deny -r 'kubectl create;kubectl apply;kubectl replace;kubectl run' -i "$CMD" -q

deny-kubectl-delete.description: Blocks resource removal: kubectl delete, drain, taint. Requires bash-deny and jq on PATH; no-ops if either is missing.
deny-kubectl-delete.shell: CMD=$(printf '%s' "$PI_TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r '.command // empty'); [ -z "$CMD" ] || bash-deny -r 'kubectl delete;kubectl drain;kubectl taint' -i "$CMD" -q

deny-kubectl-mutate.description: Blocks in-place mutations: kubectl edit, scale, patch, rollout. Requires bash-deny and jq on PATH; no-ops if either is missing.
deny-kubectl-mutate.shell: CMD=$(printf '%s' "$PI_TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r '.command // empty'); [ -z "$CMD" ] || bash-deny -r 'kubectl edit;kubectl scale;kubectl patch;kubectl rollout' -i "$CMD" -q

deny-kubectl-exec.description: Blocks running commands inside a pod (kubectl exec). Requires bash-deny and jq on PATH; no-ops if either is missing.
